json_data.title: Preparation for the Major
json_data.description: Complete the equivalent to the sixth level of one foreign language or the equivalent to the third level of two different foreign languages.
courses: ENGL 10C - Literatures in English, 1850 to Present

json_data.title: The Major
json_data.description: Complete five courses. Only one course from Linguistics 165A or 165B may be applied.

options.title: Linguistics Core
options.description: Students may substitute courses 200A or 200B for 165A or 165B respectively if they receive grades of A in 120A or 120B respectively and have consent of instructor.

options.title: English
courses: ENGL 120 - History of Aesthetics and Critical Theory
ENGL 141B - Introduction to Old English Language and Literature
LING 170 - Language and Society: Introduction to Sociolinguistics

options.title: Additional English Electives
courses: ENGL 120 - History of Aesthetics and Critical Theory
ENGL 140B - Chaucer: "Troilus and Criseyde" and Selected Minor Works
ENGL 141B - Introduction to Old English Language and Literature
ENGL 150A - Shakespeare: Poems and Early Plays

options.title: Linguistics 198A and 198B
courses: LING 198A - Honors Research in Linguistics I
LING 198B - Honors Research in Linguistics II

options.title: Linguistics 199
courses: LING 199 - Directed Research or Senior Project in Linguistics

options.title: Computer Science
courses: COM SCI 31 - Introduction to Computer Science I
COM SCI 32 - Introduction to Computer Science II

options.title: Linguistics Elective
courses: LING 180 - Mathematical Structures in Language I
